name: Cookie Pinwheels
recipeInstructions:
Prepare Pastry: Beat butter and cream cheese in a large bowl with an electric mixer until creamy. Beat in sour cream. Stir in flour and salt until firm dough forms. Add more flour if necessary. Divide dough into two halves. Flatten each half into a 4-inch square. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
Prepare Filling: Combine nuts, sugar, cinnamon, egg, and orange rind in a small bowl.
Roll out half of the pastry on a floured surface to a 9-inch square about 1/4 inch thick. Spread 3/4 cup of the filling evenly over the pastry. Roll up jelly-roll style. Wrap in foil. Refrigerate for at least 3 hours. Repeat with remaining pastry and filling.
Preheat Oven: Preheat oven to moderate (350°F). Lightly grease 2 large cookie sheets.
Cut rolls with a serrated knife into 1/4-inch thick slices. Place 1/2 inch apart on cookie sheets. Reshape into rounds, if necessary. Bake in the preheated moderate oven (350°F) for 15-20 minutes or until firm and golden brown. Cool on cookie sheets for 10 minutes. Remove cookies to wire racks to cool completely.
